questions.question_text: Which chamber of the human heart is responsible for pumping oxygenated blood to the rest of the body?
questions.explanation: The left ventricle has the thickest muscular wall and pumps oxygenated blood through the aorta to the systemic circulation.

questions.question_text: Describe the primary function of the SA (sinoatrial) node in the heart.
questions.explanation: The SA node is the natural pacemaker of the heart. It initiates the electrical impulses that determine the heart rate and coordinates the contraction of the heart muscle.

questions.question_text: Gas exchange in the lungs occurs in which of the following structures?
questions.explanation: Alveoli are tiny, balloon-shaped air sacs where oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged with the blood.
